sql_array: CREATE TABLE TestData (id INT PRIMARY KEY, text_data VARCHAR(1024), number_data INT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);

INSERT INTO TestData (id, text_data, number_data) VALUES (1, '

Test line 1

', 42), (2, NULL, 7), (3, '

Test line 3

', NULL), (4, '', 0);
SELECT id, length(text_data) AS text_length, ses_read_line(NULL) AS line FROM TestData WHERE number_data IS NULL OR number_data > 0;

SELECT id, text_data FROM TestData WHERE text_data IS NOT NULL AND ses_read_line(NULL) = '

Test line 2

' AND number_data < (SELECT AVG(number_data) FROM TestData);
WITH RankedData AS (SELECT id, text_data, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY created_at) AS rn FROM TestData) SELECT * FROM RankedData WHERE rn = 1;
SELECT * WHERE { ?s a <TestData> } UNION SELECT * FROM (SELECT ses_read_line(NULL) AS mixed_line FROM TestData WHERE text_data IS NOT NULL) AS TempData;
SELECT a.id, COUNT(b.id) FROM TestData a LEFT JOIN TestData b ON a.number_data = b.number_data GROUP BY a.id HAVING COUNT(b.id) > 0;
CREATE TABLE Metrics (metric_id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO Metrics (metric_id, value, description) VALUES (1, 3.14, 'Pi value'), (2, NULL, 'No value'), (3, (SELECT MAX(number_data) FROM TestData), 'Max number from TestData');
SELECT metric_id, value, ses_read_line(NULL) AS read_line FROM Metrics WHERE value IS NOT NULL OR description LIKE 'Pi%';

SELECT id, text_data FROM TestData WHERE text_data LIKE '%

%';
SELECT a.id FROM TestData a JOIN Metrics b ON a.id = b.metric_id WHERE ses_read_line(NULL) = a.text_data ORDER BY ses_read_line(NULL);
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE TempData AS SELECT id, text_data FROM TestData;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TempData WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Metrics WHERE Metrics.metric_id = TempData.id);
